titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Joseph Brewer Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 11055545.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday November 7, 2020 at 04:12

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains correspondence, writings, and photographs. Correspondence includes autograph and typescripts letters from Harold Acton, Iris Barry, Charles Fisk, Katherine Anne Porter, John Strachey and others. There is a small amount of outgoing letters among the correspondence. Writings include autograph manuscript and typescript drafts and printed versions of writings by Brewer, including "20th Century Typography in the Netherlands." Photographs include ones by Milton Horn and ones of Katherine Anne Porter by George Platt Lynes.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Elysium Press on the Sinclair Lewis Fund, 1999.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Joseph Brewer Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Joseph Brewer Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: Uncat ZA MS 629.

bioghist.head: Joseph Hillyer Brewer (1898-1991)
bioghist.p: Joseph Hillyer Brewer, American publisher, librarian, and president of Olivet College. He was born in 1898 and died in New York City in 1991.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, writings, and photographs. Correspondence includes autograph manuscript and typescripts letters from Harold Acton, Iris Barry, Charles Fisk, Katherine Anne Porter, John Strachey and others. There is a small amount of outgoing letters among the correspondence. Writings include autograph and typescript drafts and printed versions of writings by Brewer, including "20th Century Typography in the Netherlands." Photographs include ones by Milton Horn and ones of Katherine Anne Porter by George Platt Lynes.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Correspondence, 1922-1979. II. Other Papers, 1930-1976.

did.unittitle: Williams-Ellis, Sir Clough; Williams-Ellis, Lady Amabel; and Williams-Ellis, Susan
Correspondence relating to Ford Maddox Ford
"20th Century Typography in the Netherlands" by Joseph Brewer, autograph manuscript and typescript drafts
Photographs of Katherine Anne Porter by George Platt Lynes
